title: Tapping to Let the Sleep Battle Be Over
subtitle: A bedtime tapping practice for releasing resistance and returning to peace with the body
excerpt: A five-minute bedtime tapping practice from Day 29 of a 30-day sleep programme. The script works through the exhaustion of fighting — checking, controlling, rehearsing bad nights — and moves toward a complete surrender: body and mind wanting the same thing, and agreeing to stop resisting it.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from the Day 29 night tap of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use at bedtime when the mind is still checking, rehearsing previous bad nights, or bracing for another — when the body and mind feel like opponents rather than partners in rest.

content.text: A five-minute bedtime tapping practice from Day 29 of a 30-day sleep programme. The night tap for Day 29 works through the habit of checking and controlling at bedtime — monitoring the body for signs of failure, rehearsing bad nights before they happen. The script moves through forgiveness of the struggle and arrives at a full surrender: body and mind wanting exactly the same thing, and agreeing to stop fighting it.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though my mind still wants to check and control, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I've rehearsed every bad night before it arrives, I honour that pattern and release it now.
Even though it's been hard, I'm open to total peace with my body.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: The battle is over — I lay down my weapons.
Side of eye: I release every ounce of resistance.
Under eye: My body and I want the same thing: rest.
Under nose: We can stop fighting and start healing.
Chin: I forgive the struggle.
Collarbone: I forgive the sleepless nights.
Under arm: I forgive myself for trying so hard.
Top of head: I'm free to rest in peace now.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I'm safe to rest, fully and completely.
Side of eye: My body knows what to do.
Under eye: I breathe, I soften, I trust.
Under nose: This is what surrender feels like.
Chin: Peace washes through me like quiet water.
Collarbone: I'm whole again — mind, body, and breath.
Under arm: The war is truly over.
Top of head: I fall asleep in absolute peace.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from the Day 29 night tap of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
